summary: MDM4U one-variable statistics problems summarize a data set with a measure of centre (mean) and a measure of spread (standard deviation σ = √(Σ(x−μ)²/N)).
method: Add the values and divide by N to get the mean μ.
Subtract the mean from each value to get the deviations.
Square the deviations and average them to get the variance.
Take the square root for the standard deviation σ.
Report centre (mean) and spread (σ) together — neither alone describes the data.

example.title: Mean and standard deviation of a data set
example.find: the mean and the (population) standard deviation
